slides.title: The purple carb behind a *long life*
slides.sub: For generations, the Okinawan diet leaned on purple sweet potato. It may be one of the world's quietest longevity foods.

slides.title: A staple, not a side
slides.body: Traditional Okinawan meals were built around purple sweet potato. It was *the main carb*, not an occasional treat on the plate.

slides.title: Filling, not heavy
slides.body: Sweet potato is low in calorie density. You can eat a satisfying amount while *keeping calories modest*, which can support a healthy weight.

slides.title: Pigment with a purpose
slides.body: That deep purple comes from anthocyanins, the same antioxidants found in berries. They are *linked to* lower inflammation over time.

slides.title: One piece of a bigger habit
slides.body: No single food makes a long life. It sits inside a plant-forward, mostly whole-food pattern that *can support healthy aging*.

slides.title: An easy swap
slides.body: Trade a refined-carb side for a roasted sweet potato. Small, repeatable swaps are *what actually stick* across the years.

slides.title: Eat for the *long game*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns the food, sleep, movement and steps behind healthy aging into one daily Health Score, so good habits are easy to see and keep.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The Okinawan diet centered on a humble purple sweet potato, and it traveled alongside one of the longest healthspans on earth. 🍠🌿

It is filling, low in calorie density, rich in antioxidants, and part of a mostly plant-based pattern. One small swap, repeated for years, adds up. Save this. 🔖
